questions.solutionText: Solution Given: l = 60 c m = 0.6 m , ϕ = 1.57 × 10 − 6 W b , μ 0 = 4 π × 10 − 7 Magnetic field inside solenoid: B = μ 0 n I = μ 0 N I l Flux: ϕ = B ⋅ A = μ 0 N I l ⋅ A Magnetic moment: M = N I A From the flux equation: N I A = ϕ ⋅ l μ 0 M = ϕ ⋅ l μ 0 = 1.57 × 10 − 6 × 0.6 4 π × 10 − 7 M = 1.57 × 0.6 4 π × 10 − 6 10 − 7 M = 0.942 4 × 3.14 × 10 = 0.942 12.56 × 10 M = 0.075 × 10 = 0.75 A m 2
questions.solution: Solution Given: l = 60 c m = 0.6 m , ϕ = 1.57 × 10 − 6 W b , μ 0 = 4 π × 10 − 7 Magnetic field inside solenoid: B = μ 0 n I = μ 0 N I l Flux: ϕ = B ⋅ A = μ 0 N I l ⋅ A Magnetic moment: M = N I A From the flux equation: N I A = ϕ ⋅ l μ 0 M = ϕ ⋅ l μ 0 = 1.57 × 10 − 6 × 0.6 4 π × 10 − 7 M = 1.57 × 0.6 4 π × 10 − 6 10 − 7 M = 0.942 4 × 3.14 × 10 = 0.942 12.56 × 10 M = 0.075 × 10 = 0.75 A m 2
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Given: l=60cm=0.6m, ϕ=1.57×10−6Wb, μ0=4π×10−7

Magnetic field inside solenoid: B=μ0nI=μ0NIl

Flux: ϕ=B⋅A=μ0NIl⋅A

Magnetic moment: M=NIA

From the flux equation: NIA=ϕ⋅lμ0

M=ϕ⋅lμ0=1.57×10−6×0.64π×10−7

M=1.57×0.64π×10−610−7

M=0.9424×3.14×10=0.94212.56×10

M=0.075×10=0.75Am2

questions.text: A long metallic bar of uniform cross-section is perfectly insulated laterally. Under steady-state heat conduction, the variation of temperature θ with distance x from the hot end is best represented by which figure?
questions.questionText: A long metallic bar of uniform cross-section is perfectly insulated laterally. Under steady-state heat conduction, the variation of temperature θ with distance x from the hot end is best represented by which figure?
questions.question: A long metallic bar of uniform cross-section is perfectly insulated laterally. Under steady-state heat conduction, the variation of temperature θ with distance x from the hot end is best represented by which figure?
questions.prompt: A long metallic bar of uniform cross-section is perfectly insulated laterally. Under steady-state heat conduction, the variation of temperature θ with distance x from the hot end is best represented by which figure?
questions.html: A long metallic bar of uniform cross-section is perfectly insulated laterally. Under steady-state heat conduction, the variation of temperature θ with distance x from the hot end is best represented by which figure?
questions.questionHtml: A long metallic bar of uniform cross-section is perfectly insulated laterally. Under steady-state heat conduction, the variation of temperature θ with distance x from the hot end is best represented by which figure?
questions.solutionText: Solution Under steady-state conditions, the temperature at any point along the bar remains constant with time ( d T / d t = 0 ). This means that the rate of heat flow ( Q ) entering any section of the bar is exactly equal to the rate of heat flow leaving it. According to Fourier's Law of Heat Conduction, the rate of heat flow through a material is given by: d Q d t = − K A d θ d x Where: K = Thermal conductivity of the metal (constant for a uniform bar) A = Cross-sectional area (given as uniform) d θ d x = Temperature gradient (the change in temperature with distance) Since the bar is perfectly insulated laterally, no heat escapes through the sides. Therefore, the heat current d Q d t is the same at every point along the length x . Given that K and A are also constants for a uniform metallic bar: d θ d x = − 1 K A ( d Q d t ) = constant A constant temperature gradient d θ d x implies a linear relationship between temperature ( θ ) and distance ( x ). As x increases (moving away from the hot end), θ must decrease at a constant rate. The graph of θ vs. x must be a straight line with a negative slope.

According to Fourier's Law of Heat Conduction, the rate of heat flow through a material is given by: d Q d t = − K A d θ d x Where: K = Thermal conductivity of the metal (constant for a uniform bar) A = Cross-sectional area (given as uniform) d θ d x = Temperature gradient (the change in temperature with distance) Since the bar is perfectly insulated laterally, no heat escapes through the sides. Therefore, the heat current d Q d t is the same at every point along the length x . Given that K and A are also constants for a uniform metallic bar: d θ d x = − 1 K A ( d Q d t ) = constant A constant temperature gradient d θ d x implies a linear relationship between temperature ( θ ) and distance ( x ). As x increases (moving away from the hot end), θ must decrease at a constant rate. The graph of θ vs. x must be a straight line with a negative slope.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Under steady-state conditions, the temperature at any point along the bar remains constant with time (dT/dt=0). This means that the rate of heat flow (Q) entering any section of the bar is exactly equal to the rate of heat flow leaving it.

According to Fourier's Law of Heat Conduction, the rate of heat flow through a material is given by:

dQdt=−KAdθdx

Where:
K = Thermal conductivity of the metal (constant for a uniform bar)
A = Cross-sectional area (given as uniform)
dθdx = Temperature gradient (the change in temperature with distance)

Since the bar is perfectly insulated laterally, no heat escapes through the sides. Therefore, the heat current dQdt is the same at every point along the length x.

Given that K and A are also constants for a uniform metallic bar:

dθdx=−1KA(dQdt)= constant

A constant temperature gradient dθdx implies a linear relationship between temperature (θ) and distance (x).

As x increases (moving away from the hot end), θ must decrease at a constant rate.

The graph of θ vs. x must be a straight line with a negative slope.

questions.question: A transverse wave pulse travels along a stretched string toward a fixed end. Upon reflection from this boundary, the reflected pulse will have:
questions.prompt: A transverse wave pulse travels along a stretched string toward a fixed end. Upon reflection from this boundary, the reflected pulse will have:
questions.html: A transverse wave pulse travels along a stretched string toward a fixed end. Upon reflection from this boundary, the reflected pulse will have:
questions.questionHtml: A transverse wave pulse travels along a stretched string toward a fixed end. Upon reflection from this boundary, the reflected pulse will have:
questions.solutionText: Solution When a transverse wave pulse reflects from a fixed end (rigid boundary), two things happen: 1. The pulse undergoes a phase change of 180 ∘ (inversion). 2. The direction of propagation reverses. This is because the fixed end cannot move, so by Newton's third law, it exerts an equal and opposite force, causing the reflected pulse to be inverted.

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
When a transverse wave pulse reflects from a fixed end (rigid boundary), two things happen:

1. The pulse undergoes a phase change of 180∘ (inversion).
2. The direction of propagation reverses.

This is because the fixed end cannot move, so by Newton's third law, it exerts an equal and opposite force, causing the reflected pulse to be inverted.

questions.solutionText: Solution For the shot to hit the plane, horizontal and vertical positions must match at the same time t . Horizontal motion of plane: x p = v 0 t + 1 2 a t 2 = 250 t + 10 t 2 Horizontal motion of shot: x s = u cos ⁡ 60 ∘ ⋅ t = 600 × 1 2 ⋅ t = 300 t For hit: x p = x s 250 t + 10 t 2 = 300 t 10 t 2 = 50 t ⇒ t = 5 s Altitude H = vertical position of shot at t = 5 s : H = u sin ⁡ 60 ∘ ⋅ t − 1 2 g t 2 H = 600 × 3 2 × 5 − 1 2 × 10 × 25 H = 1500 3 − 125 H = 2598 − 125 = 2473 m ^

questions.text: Which of the following molecules has a completely planar geometry where all carbon and nitrogen atoms lie in the same geometric plane?

questions.prompt: Which of the following molecules has a completely planar geometry where all carbon and nitrogen atoms lie in the same geometric plane?
questions.html: Which of the following molecules has a completely planar geometry where all carbon and nitrogen atoms lie in the same geometric plane?
questions.questionHtml: Which of the following molecules has a completely planar geometry where all carbon and nitrogen atoms lie in the same geometric plane?
questions.solutionText: Solution (1) Biphenyl (gas phase): Two phenyl rings are twisted (not coplanar) due to steric repulsion between ortho hydrogens. Not fully planar. (2) Cyclohexane: s p 3 carbons → tetrahedral geometry → chair conformation. Not planar. (3) Tetracyanoethylene C 2 ( C N ) 4 : Central C = C is s p 2 . Each carbon is bonded to two − C N groups. The − C N groups have s p hybridized carbon and nitrogen with linear geometry ( C ≡ N ). All atoms are s p 2 or s p → entire molecule is completely planar with all carbon and nitrogen atoms in the same plane. (4) 2,3-Dimethylbut-2-ene: The methyl groups have s p 3 carbons which are tetrahedral. Not all carbons are coplanar.
questions.solution: Solution (1) Biphenyl (gas phase): Two phenyl rings are twisted (not coplanar) due to steric repulsion between ortho hydrogens. Not fully planar. (2) Cyclohexane: s p 3 carbons → tetrahedral geometry → chair conformation. Not planar. (3) Tetracyanoethylene C 2 ( C N ) 4 : Central C = C is s p 2 . Each carbon is bonded to two − C N groups. The − C N groups have s p hybridized carbon and nitrogen with linear geometry ( C ≡ N ). All atoms are s p 2 or s p → entire molecule is completely planar with all carbon and nitrogen atoms in the same plane. (4) 2,3-Dimethylbut-2-ene: The methyl groups have s p 3 carbons which are tetrahedral. Not all carbons are coplanar.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
(1) Biphenyl (gas phase): Two phenyl rings are twisted (not coplanar) due to steric repulsion between ortho hydrogens. Not fully planar.

(2) Cyclohexane: sp3 carbons → tetrahedral geometry → chair conformation. Not planar.

(3) Tetracyanoethylene C2(CN)4: Central C=C is sp2. Each carbon is bonded to two −CN groups. The −CN groups have sp hybridized carbon and nitrogen with linear geometry (C≡N). All atoms are sp2 or sp → entire molecule is completely planar with all carbon and nitrogen atoms in the same plane.

(4) 2,3-Dimethylbut-2-ene: The methyl groups have sp3 carbons which are tetrahedral. Not all carbons are coplanar.

questions.questionHtml: When one or more hydrogen atoms of an aromatic hydrocarbon are replaced by the corresponding number of halogen atoms, the resulting compounds are called as _____.
questions.solutionText: Solution Aryl halide is an aromatic compound in which one or more hydrogen atoms, directly bonded to an aromatic ring, are replaced by a halide. Aromatic hydrocarbons undergo electrophilic substitution reaction characteristically. When the aromatic hydrocarbons are reacted with halogens in the presence of Lewis acid, the hydrogen atom of the aromatic hydrocarbon is replaced by a halogen atom. The resulting compound formed is a haloarene, or can be called as a halogen derivative of arenes.

questions.html: Which of the following forms micelles in aqueous solution above certain concentration?
questions.questionHtml: Which of the following forms micelles in aqueous solution above certain concentration?
questions.solutionText: Solution Micelles are formed by surfactants — molecules with a long hydrophobic tail and a hydrophilic head — above a certain concentration called the Critical Micelle Concentration (CMC). (1) Dodecyltrimethylammonium chloride: Has a long C 12 alkyl chain (hydrophobic tail) and a quaternary ammonium head (hydrophilic). This is a cationic surfactant → forms micelles. (2) Glucose: Small, fully hydrophilic molecule. No surfactant character. (3) Urea: Small, hydrophilic molecule. No surfactant character. (4) Pyridinium chloride: No long hydrophobic chain. Not a surfactant.

questions.questionHtml: Identify the dicarboxylic acid that is least reactive towards dehydrating agent to form an anhydride.
questions.solutionText: Solution (1) Phthalic Acid: Very reactive; groups are locked in the ortho position on a rigid ring. (2) trans-Cyclohexane-1,2-dicarboxylic acid: Least Reactive. The trans orientation on a rigid ring forces the groups to point in opposite directions, making ring closure geometrically impossible without bond breaking. (3) Succinic Acid: Highly reactive; flexible chain easily forms a stable 5-membered ring. (4) Adipic Acid: Less reactive than succinic but more reactive than the trans-isomer; forms a 7-membered ring.

questions.subject: English & Logical Reasoning
questions.text: Paragraph: When Jamaican-born social activist Marcus Garvey came to the United States in 1916, he arrived at precisely the right historical moment. What made the moment right was the return of African American soldiers from the First World War in 1918, which created an ideal constituency for someone with Garvey's message of unity, pride, and improved conditions for African American communities. Hoping to participate in the traditional American ethos of individual success, many African American people entered the armed forces with enthusiasm, only to find themselves segregated from white troops and subjected to numerous indignities. They returned to a United States that was as segregated as it had been before the war. Considering similar experiences, anthropologist Anthony F. C. Wallace has argued that when a perceptible gap arises between a culture's expectations and the reality of that culture, the resulting tension can inspire a revitalization movement: an organized, conscious effort to construct a culture that fulfills longstanding expectations. Some scholars have argued that Garvey created the consciousness from which he built, in the 1920 s, the largest revitalization movement in African American history. But such an argument only tends to obscure the consciousness of identity, strength, and sense of history that already existed in the African American community. Garvey did not create this consciousness; rather, he gave this consciousness its political expression. Question: According to the passage, which of the following contributed to Marcus Garvey's success?

Some scholars have argued that Garvey created the consciousness from which he built, in the 1920 s, the largest revitalization movement in African American history. But such an argument only tends to obscure the consciousness of identity, strength, and sense of history that already existed in the African American community. Garvey did not create this consciousness; rather, he gave this consciousness its political expression.

Question:
According to the passage, which of the following contributed to Marcus Garvey's success?
questions.solutionText: Solution The passage states that Garvey "did not create this consciousness; rather, he gave this consciousness its political expression." It also mentions that the consciousness of identity, strength, and sense of history "already existed in the African American community." This means Garvey's success was because his audience had already formed a consciousness that made them receptive to his message. He arrived at the right moment when African Americans, returning from war and facing segregation, were ready for his message of unity and pride.

questions.html: If 6th March 2012 is Sunday, what will be the day of the week on 6th March 2016?
questions.questionHtml: If 6th March 2012 is Sunday, what will be the day of the week on 6th March 2016?
questions.solutionText: Solution From 6th March 2012 to 6th March 2016 = 4 years Number of odd days: 2012-2013: 2013 is not a leap year → 1 odd day 2013-2014: 2014 is not a leap year → 1 odd day 2014-2015: 2015 is not a leap year → 1 odd day 2015-2016: 2016 is a leap year, but we count from March to March, so Feb 29, 2016 is included → 2 odd days Total odd days = 1 + 1 + 1 + 2 = 5 Sunday + 5 days = Friday
questions.solution: Solution From 6th March 2012 to 6th March 2016 = 4 years Number of odd days: 2012-2013: 2013 is not a leap year → 1 odd day 2013-2014: 2014 is not a leap year → 1 odd day 2014-2015: 2015 is not a leap year → 1 odd day 2015-2016: 2016 is a leap year, but we count from March to March, so Feb 29, 2016 is included → 2 odd days Total odd days = 1 + 1 + 1 + 2 = 5 Sunday + 5 days = Friday
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
From 6th March 2012 to 6th March 2016 = 4 years

Number of odd days:
2012-2013: 2013 is not a leap year → 1 odd day
2013-2014: 2014 is not a leap year → 1 odd day
2014-2015: 2015 is not a leap year → 1 odd day
2015-2016: 2016 is a leap year, but we count from March to March, so Feb 29, 2016 is included → 2 odd days

Total odd days =1+1+1+2=5

Sunday +5 days = Friday

questions.solutionText: Solution Two bathrooms are available. To minimize total time, we need to distribute the 6 girls between 2 bathrooms such that the total time on each bathroom is as equal as possible. Times: 9, 11, 13, 18, 22, 23 minutes Total = 96 minutes, ideal split = 48 minutes each. Finding the best split: Bathroom 1: 23 + 22 = 45 minutes Bathroom 2: 18 + 13 + 11 + 9 = 51 minutes Or: Bathroom 1: 23 + 13 + 11 = 47 minutes Bathroom 2: 22 + 18 + 9 = 49 minutes Or: Bathroom 1: 23 + 13 + 9 = 45 minutes Bathroom 2: 22 + 18 + 11 = 51 minutes Or: Bathroom 1: 22 + 13 + 11 = 46 minutes Bathroom 2: 23 + 18 + 9 = 50 minutes Or: Bathroom 1: 23 + 18 + 9 = 50 minutes Bathroom 2: 22 + 13 + 11 = 46 minutes Or: Bathroom 1: 22 + 18 = 40 Bathroom 2: 23 + 13 + 11 + 9 = 56 Best split: 47 and 49 → max = 49 minutes Or: Bathroom 1: 23 + 11 + 13 = 47, Bathroom 2: 22 + 18 + 9 = 49 The last girl finishes at 49 minutes after 7:00 AM. Earliest breakfast time = 7 : 00 + 49 min = 7 : 49 AM

Bathroom 2: 18 + 13 + 11 + 9 = 51 minutes

questions.solutionText: Solution x = log ⁡ [ cot ⁡ ( π 4 + θ ) ] e x = cot ⁡ ( π 4 + θ ) cot ⁡ ( π 4 + θ ) = cos ⁡ ( π 4 + θ ) sin ⁡ ( π 4 + θ ) = 1 − tan ⁡ θ 1 + tan ⁡ θ So e x = 1 − tan ⁡ θ 1 + tan ⁡ θ e − x = 1 + tan ⁡ θ 1 − tan ⁡ θ sinh ⁡ x = e x − e − x 2 = 1 2 [ 1 − tan ⁡ θ 1 + tan ⁡ θ − 1 + tan ⁡ θ 1 − tan ⁡ θ ] = 1 2 [ ( 1 − tan ⁡ θ ) 2 − ( 1 + tan ⁡ θ ) 2 ( 1 + tan ⁡ θ ) ( 1 − tan ⁡ θ ) ] Numerator: ( 1 − 2 tan ⁡ θ + tan 2 ⁡ θ ) − ( 1 + 2 tan ⁡ θ + tan 2 ⁡ θ ) = − 4 tan ⁡ θ Denominator: 1 − tan 2 ⁡ θ sinh ⁡ x = 1 2 ⋅ − 4 tan ⁡ θ 1 − tan 2 ⁡ θ = − 2 tan ⁡ θ 1 − tan 2 ⁡ θ Since tan ⁡ 2 θ = 2 tan ⁡ θ 1 − tan 2 ⁡ θ : sinh ⁡ x = − tan ⁡ 2 θ
questions.solution: Solution x = log ⁡ [ cot ⁡ ( π 4 + θ ) ] e x = cot ⁡ ( π 4 + θ ) cot ⁡ ( π 4 + θ ) = cos ⁡ ( π 4 + θ ) sin ⁡ ( π 4 + θ ) = 1 − tan ⁡ θ 1 + tan ⁡ θ So e x = 1 − tan ⁡ θ 1 + tan ⁡ θ e − x = 1 + tan ⁡ θ 1 − tan ⁡ θ sinh ⁡ x = e x − e − x 2 = 1 2 [ 1 − tan ⁡ θ 1 + tan ⁡ θ − 1 + tan ⁡ θ 1 − tan ⁡ θ ] = 1 2 [ ( 1 − tan ⁡ θ ) 2 − ( 1 + tan ⁡ θ ) 2 ( 1 + tan ⁡ θ ) ( 1 − tan ⁡ θ ) ] Numerator: ( 1 − 2 tan ⁡ θ + tan 2 ⁡ θ ) − ( 1 + 2 tan ⁡ θ + tan 2 ⁡ θ ) = − 4 tan ⁡ θ Denominator: 1 − tan 2 ⁡ θ sinh ⁡ x = 1 2 ⋅ − 4 tan ⁡ θ 1 − tan 2 ⁡ θ = − 2 tan ⁡ θ 1 − tan 2 ⁡ θ Since tan ⁡ 2 θ = 2 tan ⁡ θ 1 − tan 2 ⁡ θ : sinh ⁡ x = − tan ⁡ 2 θ
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
x=log⁡[cot⁡(π4+θ)]

ex=cot⁡(π4+θ)

cot⁡(π4+θ)=cos⁡(π4+θ)sin⁡(π4+θ)=1−tan⁡θ1+tan⁡θ

So ex=1−tan⁡θ1+tan⁡θ

e−x=1+tan⁡θ1−tan⁡θ

sinh⁡x=ex−e−x2

=12[1−tan⁡θ1+tan⁡θ−1+tan⁡θ1−tan⁡θ]

=12[(1−tan⁡θ)2−(1+tan⁡θ)2(1+tan⁡θ)(1−tan⁡θ)]

Numerator: (1−2tan⁡θ+tan2⁡θ)−(1+2tan⁡θ+tan2⁡θ)=−4tan⁡θ

Denominator: 1−tan2⁡θ

sinh⁡x=12⋅−4tan⁡θ1−tan2⁡θ=−2tan⁡θ1−tan2⁡θ

Since tan⁡2θ=2tan⁡θ1−tan2⁡θ:

sinh⁡x=−tan⁡2θ

questions.solutionText: Solution The slope of a line a x + b y + c = 0 is m = - a b . Thus, the slope of the line x - y = 2 is m = - 1 - 1 = 1 and we know that if two lines are parallel then their slopes are equal, hence the slope of the tangent is also m = 1 . The given hyperbola is x 2 5 - y 2 4 = 1 The equation of the tangent to the hyperbola x 2 a 2 - y 2 b 2 = 1 having slope m is y = m x ± a 2 m 2 - b 2 . Hence, the equation of its tangent having slope 1 is y = x ± 5 × 1 2 - 4 ⇒ y = x ± 1 Thus, the tangents are x - y + 1 = 0 & x - y - 1 = 0 .

Thus, the tangents are x-y+1=0 & x-y-1=0.

questions.solutionText: Solution cot 70 ° + 4 cos 70 ° = cos 70 ° + 4 sin 70 ° · cos 70 ° sin 70 ° Using 2 sin A cos A = sin 2 A , we get = cos 70 ° + 2 sin 140 ° sin 70 ° = cos 90 ° - 70 ° + 2 sin 180 ° - 40 ° sin 70 ° Using, cos 90 ° - θ = sin θ , sin 180 ° - θ = sin θ , we get = sin 20 ° + 2 sin 40 ° sin 70 ° = sin 20 ° + sin 40 ° + sin 40 ° sin 70 ° Now, using sin C + sin D = 2 sin C + D 2 cos C - D 2 , we get = 2 sin 30 ° cos 10 ° + sin 40 ° sin 70 ° = 2 × 1 2 × cos 90 ° - 10 ° + sin 40 ° sin 70 ° Again, using cos 90 ° - θ = sin θ , we get = sin 80 ° + sin 40 ° sin 70 ° Again, using sin C + sin D = 2 sin C + D 2 cos C - D 2 , we get = 2 sin 60 ° cos 20 ° sin 70 ° = 2 × 3 2 × cos 90 - 70 ° sin 70 ° = 3 · sin 70 ° sin 70 ° = 3 .
questions.solution: Solution cot 70 ° + 4 cos 70 ° = cos 70 ° + 4 sin 70 ° · cos 70 ° sin 70 ° Using 2 sin A cos A = sin 2 A , we get = cos 70 ° + 2 sin 140 ° sin 70 ° = cos 90 ° - 70 ° + 2 sin 180 ° - 40 ° sin 70 ° Using, cos 90 ° - θ = sin θ , sin 180 ° - θ = sin θ , we get = sin 20 ° + 2 sin 40 ° sin 70 ° = sin 20 ° + sin 40 ° + sin 40 ° sin 70 ° Now, using sin C + sin D = 2 sin C + D 2 cos C - D 2 , we get = 2 sin 30 ° cos 10 ° + sin 40 ° sin 70 ° = 2 × 1 2 × cos 90 ° - 10 ° + sin 40 ° sin 70 ° Again, using cos 90 ° - θ = sin θ , we get = sin 80 ° + sin 40 ° sin 70 ° Again, using sin C + sin D = 2 sin C + D 2 cos C - D 2 , we get = 2 sin 60 ° cos 20 ° sin 70 ° = 2 × 3 2 × cos 90 - 70 ° sin 70 ° = 3 · sin 70 ° sin 70 ° = 3 .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

cot70°+4cos70°

=cos70°+4sin70°·cos70°sin70°

Using 2sinAcosA=sin2A, we get

=cos70°+2sin140°sin70°

=cos90°-70°+2sin180°-40°sin70°

Using, cos90°-θ=sinθ, sin180°-θ=sinθ, we get

=sin20°+2sin40°sin70°

=sin20°+sin40°+sin40°sin70°

Now, using sinC+sinD=2sinC+D2cosC-D2, we get

=2sin30°cos10°+sin40°sin70°

=2×12×cos90°-10°+sin40°sin70°

Again, using cos90°-θ=sinθ, we get

=sin80°+sin40°sin70°

Again, using sinC+sinD=2sinC+D2cosC-D2, we get

=2sin60°cos20°sin70°

=2×32×cos90-70°sin70°

=3·sin70°sin70°=3.

questions.questionHtml: The number of roots of the equation sin2x+π18·cos2x-π9=-14 lying in 0,2π is:
questions.solutionText: Solution Given, sin 2 x + π 18 · cos 2 x - π 9 = - 1 4 Multiplying LHS and RHS by 2 , ⇒ 2 sin 2 x + π 18 · cos 2 x - 2 π 18 = - 1 2 Applying, 2 sin A cos B = sin ( A + B ) + sin ( A - B ) ⇒ sin 2 x + π 18 + 2 x - 2 π 18 + sin 2 x + π 18 - 2 x + 2 π 18 = - 1 2 ⇒ sin 4 x - π 18 + sin π 6 = - 1 2 ⇒ sin 4 x - π 18 + 1 2 = - 1 2 ⇒ sin 4 x - π 18 = - 1 Now, general solution of sin x = - 1 is 4 n - 1 π 2 , n ∈ ℤ ⇒ 4 x - π 18 = ( 4 n - 1 ) π 2 , where n ∈ ℤ For n = 1 , 4 x - π 18 = 3 π 2 ⇒ 4 x = 14 π 9 ⇒ x = 7 π 18 Similarly we can find other values of ' x ' for different values of ' n ' . ∴ In 0 , 2 π there are four solutions.
questions.solution: Solution Given, sin 2 x + π 18 · cos 2 x - π 9 = - 1 4 Multiplying LHS and RHS by 2 , ⇒ 2 sin 2 x + π 18 · cos 2 x - 2 π 18 = - 1 2 Applying, 2 sin A cos B = sin ( A + B ) + sin ( A - B ) ⇒ sin 2 x + π 18 + 2 x - 2 π 18 + sin 2 x + π 18 - 2 x + 2 π 18 = - 1 2 ⇒ sin 4 x - π 18 + sin π 6 = - 1 2 ⇒ sin 4 x - π 18 + 1 2 = - 1 2 ⇒ sin 4 x - π 18 = - 1 Now, general solution of sin x = - 1 is 4 n - 1 π 2 , n ∈ ℤ ⇒ 4 x - π 18 = ( 4 n - 1 ) π 2 , where n ∈ ℤ For n = 1 , 4 x - π 18 = 3 π 2 ⇒ 4 x = 14 π 9 ⇒ x = 7 π 18 Similarly we can find other values of ' x ' for different values of ' n ' . ∴ In 0 , 2 π there are four solutions.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Given,

sin2x+π18·cos2x-π9=-14

Multiplying LHS and RHS by 2,

⇒2sin2x+π18·cos2x-2π18=-12

Applying, 2sinAcosB=sin(A+B)+sin(A-B)

⇒sin2x+π18+2x-2π18+sin2x+π18-2x+2π18=-12

⇒sin4x-π18+sinπ6=-12

⇒sin4x-π18+12=-12

⇒sin4x-π18=-1

Now, general solution of sinx=-1 is 4n-1π2, n∈ℤ

⇒4x-π18=(4n-1)π2, where n∈ℤ

For n=1, 4x-π18=3π2

⇒4x=14π9

⇒x=7π18

Similarly we can find other values of 'x' for different values of 'n'.

∴ In 0,2π there are four solutions.

questions.solutionText: Solution We are given: lim n → ∞ − 3 n + ( − 1 ) n 4 n − ( − 1 ) n Step 1: Understand the behavior of ( − 1 ) n - If n is even, ( − 1 ) n = + 1 - If n is odd, ( − 1 ) n = − 1 So, let's compute the limit separately for even and odd values of n : Case 1: n is even Then ( − 1 ) n = 1 , so the expression becomes: − 3 n + 1 4 n − 1 Divide numerator and denominator by n : − 3 + 1 n 4 − 1 n → − 3 4 as n → ∞ Case 2: n is odd Then ( − 1 ) n = − 1 , so the expression becomes: − 3 n − 1 4 n + 1 Again, divide numerator and denominator by n : − 3 − 1 n 4 + 1 n → − 3 4 as n → ∞ Conclusion: In both even and odd cases, the limit is: − 3 4

limn→∞−3n+(−1)n4n−(−1)n

Then (−1)n=1, so the expression becomes:
−3n+14n−1
Divide numerator and denominator by n :
−3+1n4−1n→−34 as n→∞

Conclusion:
In both even and odd cases, the limit is:
−34

questions.solutionText: Solution Given relation R such that R = { x , y ∈ W × W | the world x and y have atleast one letter in common} Where W denotes set of words in English dictionary Clearly x , x ∈ R ∀ x ∈ W ∴ x , x has every letter common ∴ R is reflexive Let x , y ∈ R then y x ∈ R as x and y have atleast one letter in common. ⇒ R ⁡ is symmetric. But R is not transitive ∴ Let x = DON, y = NEST, z = SHE Then x , y ∈ R and y , z ∈ R . But x , z ∉ R . ∴ R is reflexive, symmetric but not transitive.

But R is not transitive ∴ Let x = DON, y = NEST, z = SHE Then x , y ∈ R and y , z ∈ R . But x , z ∉ R . ∴ R is reflexive, symmetric but not transitive.

questions.solutionText: Solution The given line is 1 2 x - 1 = - y = z + 2 ∴ x - 1 2 = y - 0 - 1 = z + 2 1 = λ ∴ Coordinates of any point on the given line are 2 λ + 1 , - λ , λ - 2 On substituting the point 2 λ + 1 , - λ , λ - 2 , on the given plane 2 x + y - 3 z = 4 , we get 2 2 λ + 1 + - λ - 3 λ - 2 = 4 ⇒ 8 = 4 (not possible) Hence, the point 2 λ + 1 , - λ , λ - 2 cannot lie on the given plane. The given line is parallel to the given plane. There is no point of intersection of line and the plane. Hence, the direction cosines of the projected line are same as the direction cosine of the given line. ∴ Required direction cosines = 2 6 , - 1 6 , 1 6

The given line is parallel to the given plane. There is no point of intersection of line and the plane. 22λ+1+-λ-3λ-2=4

⇒8=4 (not possible)

questions.solutionText: Solution Let given series is S = cot - 1 2 + cot - 1 8 + cot - 1 18 + cot - 1 32 + . . . S = cot - 1 2 ( 1 ) 2 + cot - 1 2 ( 2 ) 2 + cot - 1 2 ( 3 ) 2 + cot - 1 2 ( 4 ) 2 + . . . Let T n = cot - 1 ( 2 n 2 ) ⇒ T n = tan - 1 1 2 n 2 = tan -1 2 n + 1 - 2 n - 1 1 + 2 n + 1 2 n - 1 = tan - 1 ( 2 n + 1 ) - tan - 1 ( 2 n - 1 ) ∴ T 1 = tan - 1 3 - tan - 1 1 T 2 = tan - 1 5 - tan - 1 3 T 3 = tan - 1 7 - tan - 1 5 ... and so on. ∴ S n = tan - 1 ( 2 n + 1 ) - tan - 1 1 ∴ lim n → ∞ S n = tan - 1 ∞ - π 4 = π 2 - π 4 = π 4 Hence, lim n → ∞ S n = π 4
questions.solution: Solution Let given series is S = cot - 1 2 + cot - 1 8 + cot - 1 18 + cot - 1 32 + . . . S = cot - 1 2 ( 1 ) 2 + cot - 1 2 ( 2 ) 2 + cot - 1 2 ( 3 ) 2 + cot - 1 2 ( 4 ) 2 + . . . Let T n = cot - 1 ( 2 n 2 ) ⇒ T n = tan - 1 1 2 n 2 = tan -1 2 n + 1 - 2 n - 1 1 + 2 n + 1 2 n - 1 = tan - 1 ( 2 n + 1 ) - tan - 1 ( 2 n - 1 ) ∴ T 1 = tan - 1 3 - tan - 1 1 T 2 = tan - 1 5 - tan - 1 3 T 3 = tan - 1 7 - tan - 1 5 ... and so on. ∴ S n = tan - 1 ( 2 n + 1 ) - tan - 1 1 ∴ lim n → ∞ S n = tan - 1 ∞ - π 4 = π 2 - π 4 = π 4 Hence, lim n → ∞ S n = π 4
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Let given series is
S=cot-12+cot-18+cot-118+cot-132 + ...
S=cot-12(1)2+cot-12(2)2+cot-12(3)2+cot-12(4)2 + ...
Let Tn=cot-1(2n2)
⇒Tn=tan-112n2
=tan-12n+1-2n-11+2n+12n-1
=tan-1(2n+1)-tan-1(2n-1)
∴ T1 = tan-13-tan-11
T2 = tan-15-tan-13
T3 = tan-17-tan-15... and so on.
∴ Sn = tan-1(2n + 1)-tan-11
∴ limn→∞Sn=tan-1∞-π4
= π 2 - π 4 = π 4

Hence, limn→∞Sn=π4

sin⁡x(sin2⁡x+sin⁡x+1)−2cos⁡x(sin2⁡x+sin⁡x+1)=0

(sin2⁡x+sin⁡x+1)(sin⁡x−2cos⁡x)=0

For sin2⁡x+sin⁡x+1=0:
Discriminant =1−4=−3<0 → No real roots.

For sin⁡x−2cos⁡x=0:
tan⁡x=2

In (−π2,π2), tan⁡x=2 has exactly one solution (since tan⁡x is monotonically increasing in this interval).
